data.title: Upexi's Solana treasury dips 7.5% post $1B shelf registration, hinting at potential increase in holdings after 5-month hiatus.
data.description: The stock of Upexi dropped by 7.5% on Tuesday following the submission of a $1 billion shelf registration. This move indicates that the company may increase its Solana holdings once more, following a period of more than five months without any new acquisitions.

data.title: Financial Star News Inc. addresses confusion from BaFin warning, clarifying unaffiliated third-party impersonation.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on December 23, 2025, Financial Star News Inc., owner of the website www.thefinancialstar.com, released a statement addressing public confusion caused by a warning from BaFin, Germany's financial regulator. The statement aims to clarify any misunderstandings surrounding the issue.

data.title: Sen. Elizabeth Warren criticizes Trump's dismissive tone on rising holiday prices due to policies. Don't be 'dramatic'.
data.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-Massachusetts) criticized President Donald Trump's response to Americans cutting back on holiday spending due to increasing prices from his administration's policies. Warren rebuked Trump's dismissive attitude when asked about people reducing their holiday purchases amid expected hikes in health insurance premiums. Trump's remarks came during an interview on Politico's The Conversation. Warren's message to Americans facing higher costs for gifts this holiday season is in direct opposition to Trump's "don't be dramatic" stance. For more details, readers can find the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Leading cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin, fell after the US economy's record growth. Bitcoin prices dropped by 1.06% to $87,619.56, Ethereum by 1.69% to $2,968.03, XRP by 1.46% to $1.87, and Dogecoin by 2.03% to $0.1300. Cryptocurrency market struggled as Bitcoin traded between $86,500 and $88,500 with a 20% rise in trading volume. Over $250 million was liquidated in the crypto market in the last 24 hours, with long position traders facing losses. Stocks like Strategy Inc. and Bitmine Immersion Technologies Inc. also saw declines.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies experienced declines on Tuesday while the stock market closed higher due to the U.S. economy's robust growth. Bitcoin dropped 1.06% to $87,619.56, Ethereum fell 1.69% to $2,968.03, XRP decreased 1.46% to $1.87, Solana dipped 2.19% to $123.57, and Dogecoin slid 2.03% to $0.1300 at 8:25 p.m. ET. Bitcoin traded between $86,500 and $88,500 with a 20% increase in trading volume, while Ethereum hovered around $3,000 awaiting a significant breakout. Over $250 million was liquidated from the cryptocurrency market in 24 hours, with long positions facing losses. Crypto-linked stocks like MSTR and COIN closed down 3.92% and 4.21%, respectively.

data.title: Toshiba released a CMOS dual comparator with high-speed response, full input/output range for overcurrent detection.
data.description: In Kawasaki, Japan, Toshiba has launched a new CMOS dual comparator boasting rapid response times and a wide I/O range, ideal for overcurrent detection in industrial machinery. This innovative product aims to enhance safety and efficiency across various sectors with its advanced features.
